characters.name: Abigail
characters.description: Abigail is an employee ofBanditosand an unfavored employee ofGail Huggins.  She does not appear on screen. Her illness and absence from work is the reasonMark'srequest for holiday is denied.

characters.description: AlanSophie,Barbara,Mikeand Alan (far right) at aJLBstaff meeting.OccupationJLBemployeePerformer DetailsPortrayed byJustin EdwardsFirstDream JobLastDream Job Alan (not to be confused withAlan Johnson) is an employee at JLB who was at the company at the time ofSophie'spromotion to regional supervisor. He is played byJustin Edwards. He was assigned to be on Sophie's team after the promotion and was atFrankie and Benny'sfor a meeting withBarbara BinghamandMike, whenMarkbarges in.

characters.name: Alan Johnson
characters.description: Alan JohnsonNicknameJohnsonBorn1973, Shropshire, EnglandOccupationupper management atMet City Bank(current),manager atJLB(former)Interestsmaking money, business, spreadsheets, tai chi, Windows VistaFamilyunnamed ex-wifePartnersBig SuzeResidenceLondon, EnglandPerformer DetailsPortrayed byPaterson JosephFirstMark Makes a FriendLastAre We Going To Be Alright? —Johnson to Mark. Alan Johnson, frequently referred to as simply 'Johnson'is the nemesis toMarkandJeremyinPeep Show. He is portrayed byPaterson Joseph. As senior loan manager, Alan Johnson befriends Mark after meeting him atJLB Credit. He is a suave and professional businessman and a sharp dresser, often seen wearing waistcoats. He is wantonly abusive of his managerial power, crass with his speech, and is generally unchallenged as a professional and social opponent. Johnson applies heartless business maxims and ideology into everyday life, viewing his 'indecent proposal' to Jeremy, requesting permission to sleep with Big Suze for £530, as no different to "hiring a solicitor or leasing out a Spanish villa." This attitude is also seen in his eulogy forGerrardinseries 8, and when he orders Mark to fire Sophie, calling JLB a "maximum security business institution." and appearing outside the window as Mark is talking to Sophie, miming a gunshot to the head.  As ofseries 2, he is effectively Mark's boss at JLB. Mark at one point, believes he is in love with Johnson, the notion of which repulses Johnson, albeit they remain associates and even near-business associates.  It is revealed that Johnson runs a mentoring program for young, black men, and engages in community work. He was previously an alcoholic for 15 years, having been "to the bottom of the bottle, and back to the top." He was also married at one point, saying that he "doesn't need another bullet in the head."Jeremyis initially friendly with Johnson, but this dissolves as soon as Johnson goes forBig Suze, and she leaves Jeremy for him. Duringseries 6, after the termination of the London JLB branch, he moves into a cheap house with Suze, referred to by Mark as his "recession residence" and shows signs of insanity due to his fall from power, including writing his name on fruit and becoming angry when Suze would eat a 'Johnson orange.' He swindles Mark out of £2,000 by attempting to make him an executive in a new management consulting firm calledConsultio/Consultius. However, he does secure a contract with a company calledSales Direct, holding a meeting with executivesColinandNazin theMexican restaurantin which Mark works. In the final episode ofseries 7,however, his fortunes seem to have improved after he hosts a house warming/New Year's Eve party at his expensive new home. He hints to Mark that his upturn in fortune is due to direct marketing/pyramid schemes. However, in a fit of rage after seeing Mark atBig Suze’s rival New Years Eve Party, he throws away his alleged sobriety by downing a glass of champagne. Johnson's fortunes appear to have

characters.description: Alan McGurnageeOccupationBath supplies retailerPerformer DetailsPortrayed byGeorge PottsFirstChairman MarkLastChairman Mark Alan McGurnagee is a sales rep for a bath supplies retailer who meets withMarkin the episodeChairman Mark.  He is played byGeorge Potts. Alan is a cheerful, polite man who seems quite agreeable albeit he does not buy anything from him despite Mark having traveled all day via bus to meet with him.

characters.name: Alice
characters.description: AliceAlice at the club withDobby.PartnersNeil(ex-husband)ResidenceGrafton ParkPerformer DetailsPortrayed byLaura Jean MarshFirstThe Love Bunker(mentioned)Quantocking II(appears)LastQuantocking II Alice is the wife ofNeilseen briefly in the club hanging out withDobbyandTrishwhen Dobby hears of her new job opportunity in New York.  She is played byLaura Jean Marsh. Apparently Alice works in the same field as Simon, Neil, and Trish.  Previously married toNeil, they break up due toJeremy'sshoddy life coaching. From what Dobby gathers, Alice is quite the "doom-monger." (The Love Bunker)

characters.name: Alison
characters.description: Alison, as mentioned bySophie, is a mum whose kid Sophie's boyfriend,Duncan, tutors.  She never appears on screen. Sophie and Alison have met before and her affair with Duncan is long presumed to have been in progress before Duncan admits to it. She drives a "fuck me" Mazda and and wears "floaty dresses," which Sophie regards with disdain.  Because of this affair, Sophie seeks a way out by offeringMark(once again) Nana's Cottage (Kid Farm).

characters.description: Annoying ManThe annoying man is seen here heckling Mark during his history walk.Performer DetailsPortrayed byRobert PopperFirstJeremy in LoveLastJeremy in Love The annoying man is a heckler who crashesMark Corrigan'shistory walk in the episodeJeremy in Love.  He is played byRobert Popper. At first it seems that Mark is in danger of handling him quite poorly, but in the end Mark diffuses the annoying man and he moves on.

characters.name: April Danecroft
characters.description: April DanecroftNicknameJoan of SnarkOccupationshoe saleswomanhistorical authorlecturer, South Thames CollegeInterestsThe Ottoman EmpireAncient HistoryAncient EgyptliteratureFamilyAngus Maynard(husband)Hayley(neice)PartnersMarkResidenceCroydonUniversity of DartmouthPerformer DetailsPortrayed byCatherine ShepherdFirstUniversity ChallengeLastAre We Going To Be Alright?April Maynard(néeDanecroft) is a romantic interest ofMark Corriganwho he meets at a shoe store, and subsequently follows toUniversity of Dartmouth.  She is played byCatherine Shepherd. Hayley(neice) April first meetsMarkwhen she is working part time as a sales associate at a shoe shop in Croydon. The two bond over jokes about ancient Egyptian units of measurement, and Mark buys a set of brogues. Infatuated by her, he returns the next day with a gift, only to discover from her manager she has returned to her studies atUniversity of Dartmouth- which is, coincidentally, Mark's alma mater. April is shy, fidgety, and nervous, but is very intelligent and beautiful. She is relatively conservative about committed relationships but carries an understanding perspective when it comes to her husband Angus' bizarre behavior.  Ultimately when her relationship with Angus is thrown into disarray, she makes quick work of Mark, sexually.

characters.name: Aurora
characters.description: AuroraInterestsdogsFamilyMalcom(father),unnamed mother,Lucy(sister)Performer DetailsPortrayed byCara HorganFirstHolidayLastHoliday Aurorais a woman who meetsMarkandJeremyin the episodeHoliday.  She is played byCara Horgan. Aurora is a Daddy's-girl — a bit childish, bratty, and spoiled. While on Mark's stag-do, Jeremy meets her and her sisterLucyhaving dinner at a local pub.  He decides to introduce himself, and Aurora takes an instant liking to him.

She was present at the firing ofMark Corriganin theseries finaleofPeep Show.

characters.description: Barbara BinghamOccupationHR manager atJLBPerformer DetailsPortrayed bySandy WalshFirstThe InterviewLastDream Job Barbara Binghamis the HR manager atJLBinSeries 1. She is portrayed by Sandy Walsh. Her first appearance is in the episodeThe Interviewand she is part of the hiring committee atJLBalong with her colleague,Lucy.She is thought to be racist (Mark's drawing for Sohpie includes a swastika, which Sophie asserts is  "so Barbara"), as well as not being particularly likeable.

characters.description: BarryBarry in Toni's flat.ResidenceApollo HousePerformer DetailsPortrayed byRobert HarleyFirstWarring FactionsLastWarring Factions Barry is a resident ofApollo Houseand friend ofToni'swho is invited to her party in the episodeWarring Factions. He drinks red wine, has a particularly harry chest, and has a particular liking for the A304. (Warring Factions)

characters.name: Beautiful Laura
characters.description: Beautiful LauraLaura asleep on the floor of Apollo House, Flat 5.NicknameBeautiful LauraInterestsclubbingPerformer DetailsPortrayed byGeorgia ZarisFirstJuryingLastJuryingLaura is a friend ofDomandOllie, two associates ofSophiewho go clubbing with her andMarkin the episodeJurying.  She is portrayed by Georgia Zaris. She seems to be quite into the idea of a gay club and party to the extreme.  On the night she goes clubbing with Mark, she ends up inebriated on his floor atApollo Houseand remains there until Mark her and her friends. (Jurying)' She is affectionately nicknamed "Beatiful Laura," by Dom.

characters.name: Beautiful Laura
characters.description: Beautiful LauraLaura asleep on the floor of Apollo House, Flat 5.NicknameBeautiful LauraInterestsclubbingPerformer DetailsPortrayed byGeorgia ZarisFirstJuryingLastJuryingLaura is a friend ofDomandOllie, two associates ofSophiewho go clubbing with her andMarkin the episodeJurying.  She is portrayed by Georgia Zaris. She seems to be quite into the idea of a gay club and party to the extreme.  On the night she goes clubbing with Mark, she ends up inebriated on his floor atApollo Houseand remains there until Mark her and her friends. (Jurying)' She is affectionately nicknamed "Beatiful Laura," by Dom.

He is played byDanny Babington. WhenJeremyis first introduced to Ben, Ben is in a coma.  Jeremy secretly wants Ben to perish so that he can be with Zahra.
